perturbation.original_text: Party A (Shipper): ZTO Express Co., Ltd. Address: Building 1, No. 1685, Huazhi Road, Huaxin Twon, Qingpu District, Shanghai
perturbation.changed_text: Party A (Client): ZTO Express Co., Ltd. Address: Building 1, No. 1685, Huazhi Road, Huaxin Twon, Qingpu District, Shanghai
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Shipper' to 'Client' introduces ambiguity. Throughout the agreement, Party A is consistently referred to as 'Party A' or 'Shipper.' This change leads to uncertainty as to whether 'Client' is synonymous with 'Shipper' and if obligations assigned to the Shipper apply to the Client.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Freight and payment method: (a) Verification of freight: Party A pays freight based on carload rate (such freight includes pick-up charges, door-to-door delivery charges and tax fees). (b) Party A shall not pay any other charges other than the freight. (c) Clearance of freight: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel EXW originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines Party A as the 'Shipper'. The changed text redefines Party A as 'Client'. The contradicted text refers to Party A's obligations regarding freight and EXW weight. Since the agreement consistently refers to 'Shipper' for payment and freight related tasks, changing 'Shipper' to 'Client' introduces ambiguity regarding which entity is responsible for those obligations. It is unclear whether obligations originally assigned to 'Shipper' also apply to the 'Client'. This introduces an in-text contradiction as 'Shipper' and 'Client' are now used interchangeably without clear definition, conflicting with obligations related to freight payments and clearance as stated in the contradicted text according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Party B (Carrier): Tonglu Tongze Logistics Ltd. Address: 12 Floor, HSBC Tower, Yinchun South Road, Tonglu County, Zhejiang Province
perturbation.changed_text: Party B (Service Provider): Tonglu Tongze Logistics Ltd. Address: 12 Floor, HSBC Tower, Yinchun South Road, Tonglu County, Zhejiang Province
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Carrier' to 'Service Provider' introduces ambiguity. Throughout the agreement, Party B is consistently referred to as 'Party B' or 'Carrier'. This change creates uncertainty about whether all obligations assigned to the 'Carrier' also apply to the 'Service Provider'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In order to guarantee rapid transfer of Party A's parcel, Party B shall strictly comply with the following obligations: (a) During the transportation, Part B shall completely comply with Party A's transportation arrangement and relevant systems. Party B shall bear any consequences and legal liability arising out of Party B's non-compliance, and Party A shall have no liability. Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement without further legal liability in the case that Party B does not comply with Party A's management and arrangement. (b) Based on the business needs, Party A can negotiate with Party B to modify the transportation route and time whenever necessary, which shall be executed by signing supplemental agreements upon consensus of both Parties. (c) Party B shall ensure the vehicles are in good conditions, the compartments are properly sealed without leakage and the vehicles are equipped with fire-fighting equipment. In the event of parcel damage resulting from leakage or fire, Party B shall indemnify at the standard rate of RMB200 per parcel, and indemnify the actual price for high-end insured parcel (or indemnify by the value of the parcel provided by arbitration department determined by Party A). (d) Party B shall have valid and legal licenses for national road transportation. In the event of loss caused to Party A by delivery delay due to vehicles detention for the lack of license, Party B shall compensate for any loss to Party A. (e) Party B shall arrive at the network partners determined by Party A according to the time and route stipulated in this Agreement, and strictly comply with the start time and end time. Unless otherwise approved by Party A, in the event of parcel transfer due to Party B's vehicle delay, Party B shall pay liquidated damages to Party A at the standard rate of RMB500 per trip on the first working day, RMB1,000 per trip on the second working day and RMB2,000 per trip on the third working day (based on the time records on the parcel transfer documents) and such liquidated damages will be deducted from the current month's freight. In the special event of changes to route and time and severe weather (or other force majeure events), Party B shall be in touch with Party A in time and record such special event on the parcel transfer documents with Party A's approval. (f) In the event of delay by vehicle malfunction or traffic accidents, Party B shall settle such malfunction or accidents within half an hour. In the event of vehicle operation failure, Party B shall notify Party A within two hours and shall manage to deliver Party A's parcel to the destination designated by the contract. (g) Party B shall provide copies of driver licenses, working licenses, occupation licenses, insurance documents, operation licenses, outsourcing contracts, tax certificates, business licenses, road transportation permits and business code 2 Source: ZTO EXPRESS (CAYMAN) INC., F-1, 9/30/2016 licenses to Party A, and guarantee the authenticity, completeness, legality and validity of such licenses and materials. Party B's drivers shall have at least two years driving experience in large trucks and have relevant licenses. Party B shall bear any consequences and legal liability arising out of Party B's non-compliance, and Party A shall have no liability. (h) Party B shall bear any consequences and economic punishments arising out of the breach of traffic rules by Party B's drivers and other staff, and Party A shall have no liability. (i) Party B shall be responsible for driving safety. Party B shall bear any legal liability arising out of severe traffic accidents causing vehicle damage and personnel casualties, and Party A shall have no liability. Party B shall be responsible for any damages resulting from severe accidents causing Party A's personnel casualties. Party A has the right to indemnify from Party B upon advance payment of damages.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes 'Carrier' to 'Service Provider' for Party B. The contradicted text consistently refers to Party B as the 'Carrier' and assigns specific obligations to the 'Carrier'. By changing the designation, it introduces ambiguity about whether these obligations apply to Party B as the 'Service Provider', creating a direct conflict and uncertainty. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition causing an in-text contradiction because the contract now uses two different terms for the same party without clarifying if they are interchangeable, leading to conflicting interpretations of the obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel EXW originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.changed_text: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and warehouse weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the term 'EX-warehouse ("EXW")' creates a contradiction, the section does not specifically refer to an EXW, it now refers to warehouse weight, the main issue with the new term is it introduces a contradiction regarding how Party A determines the weight, as it originally was stated by EXW. Changing this introduces uncertainty. Are all parcels subject to a warehouse weight determination, or are there exceptions? The original EXW definition provided clarity in this determination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clearance of freight: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and warehouse weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A', providing a specific method for weight determination. The changed text removes 'EXW', stating 'warehouse weight determined by Party A'. The contradicted text also refers to 'warehouse weight determined by Party A.' This change introduces a contradiction because it removes the defined method (EXW), potentially creating ambiguity and conflict about how weight is actually determined, which is a key factor in calculating the final clearance amount. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because removing 'EXW' leads to conflicting obligations regarding how the weight will be calculated.
